policy: Full Fundstrat transcripts are stored only in the private source vault.

items.title: Interim Deal Underpins a Constructive Framework as Industrials Break Out and Semis Rebound
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat technical-strategy video detail page showed Vimeo Transcript control; complete player-exposed VTT caption track captured from the same iframe metadata.
items.short_synthesis: Newton stayed near-term bullish as lower WTI, yields, and USD improved the cross-asset backdrop, while flagging FOMC/gap-fill resistance and emphasizing Industrials breadth plus Semiconductor support levels.

items.title: Geopolitical Tailwinds May Give Warsh Room to Lean Dovish, HYPE Reclaims Leadership
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat video detail page showed Vimeo Transcript control; visible panel was virtualized, so the complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same iframe metadata.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell framed lower crude/yields after US-Iran progress as a better liquidity backdrop for crypto, while keeping Strategy reserve coverage as the key unfinished risk and noting HYPE leadership recovery.

items.title: DXY Trends Reinforce Favorable Odds of a Tactical Rally, but Durable Lows Remain Difficult to Confirm
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed a Vimeo embed with caption/CC availability; complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same embed metadata. No separate transcript pane was visible in the Fundstrat page DOM.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell sees improved crypto risk/reward and tactical-rally odds, but says durable lows remain unconfirmed and STRC/MSTR reserve mechanics keep sleeve sizing tactical rather than aggressive.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: Wed is the debut of Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair with a new 'limited comms' style. Also, SPCX IPO successful as we expected.
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed a Vimeo embed with caption/CC availability; complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same embed metadata. No separate transcript pane was visible in the Fundstrat page DOM.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee treated Warsh first FOMC communication style as the main next-day macro checkpoint and framed the SPCX IPO as supportive of AI/private-market capital appetite, pending post-FOMC confirmation.

items.title: Warsh's First FOMC Lifts the Dollar and Rates, but the Pop Looks Exaggerated
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed a Vimeo embed with caption/CC availability; complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same embed metadata. No separate transcript pane was visible in the Fundstrat page DOM.
items.short_synthesis: Newton keeps near-term U.S. equity trends bullish after Warsh FOMC, treats the dollar/rates pop as exaggerated, and frames the SPX pullback into Fibonacci support as buyable rather than a larger breakdown.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: Warsh solid FOMC presser. Contrary to consensus, Fed is not hawkish, but moving to 'dynamically navigating' rates
items.completeness_notes: Chrome transcript pane was visible in logged-in Fundstrat Chrome. Captured 40 visible/previously exposed cues from 00:03 through 06:34 by opening the Vimeo transcript pane and keyboard-scrolling; possible minor omissions if the virtualized pane skipped hidden duplicate fragments.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee frames Warsh as not hawkish and as dynamically navigating rates, making the post-FOMC broad-equity dip a buyable/recheck setup rather than a de-risking signal.

items.title: Two Key Ways to Play the Rebound in the Consumer as Crude's Decline Continues
items.completeness_notes: Chrome/Vimeo transcript pane was visible and scroll-captured through the 07:39 ending cue; capture is practical-visible text, not guaranteed official full transcript export.
items.short_synthesis: Newton framed the June 18 close as bullish broadening: lean bullish, add on weakness while June lows hold, favor consumer rebound via JETS/DAL and XHB, keep QQQ constructive above 722, and watch SMH overbought risk despite trend strength.
